prompt: Construct a Meridian Records & Compliance Backend supporting offline music catalog operations, internal behavioral analytics, governance workflows, and secure administration on a single-node Laravel + MySQL deployment. The system exposes Music Library APIs that support create/query/update and lifecycle actions for songs, albums, and playlists, including publish/unpublish, semantic versioning (major.minor.patch), and strict metadata constraints: title and artist are required strings (1–200 chars), duration is an integer in seconds (1–7,200), audio_quality is an enum (MP3_320, FLAC_16_44, FLAC_24_96), tags are up to 20 normalized tokens (lowercase, 2–24 chars), and cover_art is stored as a local file reference with SHA-256 fingerprint and max 5 MB. Search APIs support keyword match plus filtering and sorting across artist, tag, duration range, and publish_state, with results returned in deterministic order for idempotent pagination. User Behavior APIs collect events (browse, search, click, favorite, rate, comment) with server-side timestamping, deduplication by (user_id, event_type, target_id, 5-second window), and profile modeling that maintains interest tags and preference vectors using configurable weights; time decay applies a 30-day half-life, and cold-start falls back to “popular in last 7 days” and “content-similar by shared tags/artist.” Notifications & Task Center APIs manage in-app messages and subscriptions strictly within the system (no SMS/email/push), including templates with variable placeholders, targeted/bulk delivery capped at 10,000 recipients per run, send-frequency limits of 3 per user per hour per template, and read receipts. A Rewards/Penalties module supports configurable types, disciplinary record registration, linkage to evaluation cycles and leader profiles, status transitions (active, appealed, cleared), expiration rules (default 365 days), and statistical reporting by role, period, and category. Data collection and lab-result-style validation is supported as a generalized “result entry” subsystem for structured measurements tied to a subject entity and review workflow: manual entry, CSV import, and offline REST-style integration accepting HL7/FHIR-like field semantics (code, unit, reference_range, observed_at). Validation enforces type/format rules, unit normalization, range checks, and logical consistency (e.g., observed_at cannot be in the future by more than 5 minutes); outliers are flagged using configurable z-score thresholds (default |z| ≥ 3.0) and require reviewer sign-off before finalization. The MySQL schema uses immutable event tables for behavior and notifications, normalized catalog tables for content, and audit tables capturing actor_id, action, resource_type/id, request_id, and before/after hashes; key indexes include (publish_state, updated_at), (artist, title), and (user_id, created_at) for timeline queries. Authentication is local username/password only, with bcrypt hashing, minimum 12 characters, lockout after 10 failed attempts per 15 minutes, forced logout via token revocation, and optional offline SSO explicitly limited to on-prem LDAP/Kerberos connectors without external calls. Authorization uses role-based access control with function permissions and data scopes (campus/organization, subject, and time-range), and all admin actions are audited; machine clients are supported via dedicated local service accounts with configurable throttling (default 60 requests/min per account) and credential rotation, with credentials stored encrypted at rest. Non-functional requirements include operating fully without internet, single-machine Docker deployment, p95 latency under 300 ms for indexed queries at 200 RPS, nightly local backups, structured logs correlated by request_id, and field-level masking for sensitive identifiers in logs and exports.
